mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and boosts profit margins, driving positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which forces equity prices higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation signals capacity utilization improvements that directly lift corporate earnings and push S&P 500 prices up within one week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in over 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic strength that translates into higher corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 230, inflation expectations accelerate and force discount rates higher, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the negative correlation breaks down after 2013.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory buildup and weakening consumer demand, which triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of quarterly observations, or Rank IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals output contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations, driving S&P 500 prices down within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 40 percent of cases when INDPRO falls during rate hike periods, or the signal shows no statistical edge.
